description: Order Book / Backlog – Backlog ₹214.6bn, +49% YoY, multi-year visibility
Book-to-Bill Ratio – Order inflow significantly ahead of revenue run-rate
New Contract Wins – HVDC, VSC-HVDC and multiple large project wins
Order Growth Rate – Q4 order intake +188% YoY
3-Year Revenue Forecast – Strong visibility from backlog and HVDC pipeline
EBITDA Margin Expansion (trend) – Record Q4 EBITDA margin of 27.2%
Net Profit Margin Trend – PBT more than doubled YoY
Cash & Equivalents Level – ₹25bn cash and cash equivalents, zero debt
Business Model Disruption Risk – Industry tailwinds outweigh disruption risks
Regulatory / Geopolitical / Policy Risk – Some localization and import dependency risks
Contingent Liabilities / Litigation – Wage-code provision only; no major issues highlighted
Corporate Governance Quality – Strong disclosure standards and conservative commentary
Management Quality – Excellent execution, risk management and capital allocation
Conference Call Available – Detailed and transparent management interaction
Other Factor: Backlog Quality – 98% backlog from private customers, PSUs and central utilities; SEB exposure <2%
Order Book / Backlog – Record order book of ₹14,010 cr, +66% YoY, with over two-thirds executable in FY27 and visibility extending into FY28
Book-to-Bill Ratio – Order book ~1.75x FY26 revenue, providing strong revenue visibility
New Contract Wins – Strong demand across modules and cells, with management expecting a post-ALMM-2 order surge
3-Year Revenue Forecast – ₹12,000 cr multi-year capex plan, capacity expansion across cells, wafers, batteries and inverters supports long-term growth visibility
EBITDA Margin Expansion (trend) – Operational EBITDA margin reached 30.4% despite commodity and freight inflation; operating leverage and automation benefits evident
Net Profit Margin Trend – PAT margin reached 18.8% and PAT grew 61.1% YoY, significantly ahead of revenue growth
Business Model Disruption Risk – Technology roadmap appears robust with TOPCon upgrades, Zero Busbar products and realistic assessment of next-generation technologies
Regulatory / Geopolitical / Policy Risk – ALMM-2 implementation is a major positive, though Middle East supply-chain disruptions and policy timing remain watchpoints
Corporate Governance Quality – Transparent communication on fundraising, leverage limits, acquisition strategy and technology roadmap
Management Quality – Strong execution on capacity ramps, cost control, risk pass-through mechanisms and disciplined capital allocation
Conference Call Available – Detailed disclosures across operations, policy, technology, capex and balance-sheet strategy
Other Factor: Capacity Expansion Execution – 5.6 GW module plant commissioned, 7 GW cell expansion on track, brownfield replication could lower future capex by 30–40%
Other Factor: Regulatory Tailwind (ALMM-2) – Expected shift of private solar market to domestic cells from June 1 creates a significant near-term demand catalyst
Other Factor: Diversification Strategy – Transcon acquisition, inverter plans and battery investments broaden earnings drivers beyond solar modules
